body:
Flagged by AI reviewer in PR #847.

## Problem

All four snapshot collector scripts (`snapshot-agents.sh`, `snapshot-forge.sh`, `snapshot-inbox.sh`, `snapshot-nomad.sh`) define a `mktemp_safe()` helper that appends to `TMPFILES` and registers a `trap cleanup EXIT`. However, every call site uses command substitution:

```bash
tmpfile="$(mktemp_safe /tmp/foo.XXXXXX)"
```

This runs `mktemp_safe` in a subshell. The `TMPFILES+=()` inside the subshell is discarded when the subshell exits, so the parent shell's `TMPFILES` stays empty. The `cleanup` trap then calls `rm -f` with an empty array — no files are removed on abnormal exit.

This was correctly identified and fixed for `snapshot-daemon.sh` in PR #840/#847, and the commit message explicitly notes: *"The collectors still use the buggy form — separate follow-up needed."*

## Fix

For each call site, replace:
```bash
tmpfile="$(mktemp_safe /tmp/foo.XXXXXX)"
```
with:
```bash
mktemp_safe /tmp/foo.XXXXXX
tmpfile="$_TMPFILE"
```

This mirrors the pattern used in the fixed `snapshot-daemon.sh`.

Affected call sites (approximate count):
- `snapshot-agents.sh`: 2 calls
- `snapshot-forge.sh`: 6 calls  
- `snapshot-inbox.sh`: 6 calls
- `snapshot-nomad.sh`: 1 call

---
*Auto-created from AI review of PR #847*
## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All tmpfile="$(mktemp_safe /tmp/...)" patterns replaced with mktemp_safe /tmp/... + tmpfile="$_TMPFILE" in snapshot-agents.sh
- [ ] Same pattern applied in snapshot-forge.sh
- [ ] Same pattern applied in snapshot-inbox.sh
- [ ] Same pattern applied in snapshot-nomad.sh
- [ ] TMPFILES tracking works correctly on abnormal exit (tmpfiles cleaned up)
- [ ] shellcheck passes on all four files

## Affected files
- bin/snapshot-agents.sh (2 call sites)
- bin/snapshot-forge.sh (6 call sites)
- bin/snapshot-inbox.sh (6 call sites)
- bin/snapshot-nomad.sh (1 call site)

Flagged by AI reviewer in PR #845.

## Problem

`fetch_alloc_logs()` at `bin/snapshot-agents.sh:92-94` still calls `nomad alloc logs` with the old CLI flags (`-token`, `-address`, `-timeout` with `s` suffix):

```bash
nomad alloc logs -tail="$LOG_TAIL" -address="$NOMAD_ADDR" \
  -token="$NOMAD_TOKEN" -timeout="${NOMAD_TIMEOUT}s" \
  "$alloc_id" > "$dest" 2>/dev/null || true
```

PR #845 converted `nomad job list` and `nomad alloc list` to HTTP API calls (fixing Nomad 1.9.5 compatibility), but the log-streaming command was left unchanged. If `nomad alloc logs` is also absent or flag-incompatible in the deployed Nomad version, agent state detection will silently produce stalled results for all agents.

The Nomad HTTP streaming log endpoint (`/v1/client/fs/logs/<alloc_id>`) is more complex to consume with plain curl, which is why it was likely deferred.

---
*Auto-created from AI review*
## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] fetch_alloc_logs() updated to use Nomad 1.9.5-compatible flags or HTTP API
- [ ] nomad alloc logs calls in bin/snapshot-agents.sh use correct flag format
- [ ] Snapshot log collection works against Nomad 1.9.5
- [ ] shellcheck passes

## Affected files
- bin/snapshot-agents.sh (fetch_alloc_logs function)

## Known context
- PR #845 converted nomad job/alloc list to HTTP API but left log streaming unchanged
- The nomad HTTP log streaming endpoint is complex; flag fix is the minimal viable fix
- Nomad 1.9.5 may not support the old -token/-address flags for alloc logs
